title: Fitting Finite Mixture Models for Time-to-Event Endpoints
text: Finite mixture models developed model continuous discrete values arising separate latent distributions. implemented adaptation modeling approach time--event endpoints, require additional consideration due observations censored therefore observed event time. {survFMM} package fits adaptations finite mixture model time--event endpoints.

title: Fitting Finite Mixture Models for Time-to-Event Endpoints
text: use simulated dataset provided {survFMM} package, sim_data. dataset includes data 1500 observations arising three latent subgroups. practice, values latent_subgroup unknown. purposes examples, evaluate 3 subgroups. number subgroups may pre-specified evaluated using AIC BIC (currently computed part survFMM, may future enhancement). fit IPCW-FMM AFT-FMM. weights already computed part simulated dataset. Starting values outcome model parameters based fitting single AFT model data adding random noise parameters. repeat initialization 10 times; initialization resulting largest log-likelihood selected final estimates. outcome model fit treatment covariate since models apply IPTW (required pre-computed specified function call; computed part survFMM). latent subgroup membership model identical IPCW-FMM AFT-FMM, multinomial logistic regression model covariates covariate_sim_normal covariate_sim_binary1. allow model converge change log-likelihood less 0.001 (default) fewer 3% observations change latent subgroup.

title: Fitting Finite Mixture Models for Time-to-Event Endpoints
text: simulated data, 30% observations censored (70% event).  However, since latent subgroup membership known practice, compare survival across treatment arms, observe following:
code: tbl_summary(sim_data,             by = latent_subgroup,             include = c(tx, covariate_sim_normal, covariate_sim_binary1,                         event_status)) %>%    bold_labels() %>%   modify_header(all_stat_cols() ~ "**Subgroup {level}**  \nN = {n} ({round(100*p)}%)") %>%    add_overall() # KM curve by treatment and latent subgroup km_tx_subgroup <- survfit2(Surv(time_to_event_days, event_status) ~ latent_subgroup + tx,                            data = sim_data %>%                               mutate(latent_subgroup = paste0("Subgroup ", latent_subgroup),                                     tx = paste0("Tx ", tx))) ggsurvfit(km_tx_subgroup, linetype_aes = TRUE) +   add_confidence_interval() +   add_censor_mark() +   scale_color_manual(values = c('#F8766D', '#F8766D',                                 '#54738E', '#54738E',                                 '#82AC7C', '#82AC7C'                                 )) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c('#F8766D', '#F8766D',                                 '#54738E', '#54738E',                                 '#82AC7C', '#82AC7C')) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = rep(c(1, 2), 3)) +   labs(x = "Time from Treatment Initiation") # KM curve by treatment km_tx <- survfit2(Surv(time_to_event_days, event_status) ~ tx,                  data = sim_data) ggsurvfit(km_tx) +   add_confidence_interval() +   add_censor_mark() +   labs(color = "Treatment",        fill = "Treatment",        x = "Time from Treatment Initiation")

title: Fitting Finite Mixture Models for Time-to-Event Endpoints
text: can start inverse probability censoring weighted version FMM (IPCW-FMM), applied among individuals event weighted probability included (.e., censored). can now fit model, rather fit model among events apply IPCW, can utilize observations fitting mixture accelerated failure time models (AFT-FMM). differences calls survFMM() model weights; IPCW-FMM requires IPTW*IPCW, whereas AFT-FMM requires IPTW.
code: set.seed(1234)  ex_ipcw_fmm <- survFMM(                  model = "ipcw-fmm",                  input_df = sim_data,                  weights = "iptw_ipcw_trim97",                  outc_distribution = "weibull",                  outc_model_time = "time_to_event_days",                  outc_model_status = "event_status",                  outc_model_covars = "tx",                  k = 3,                  starting_values_type = "single_survreg",                  starting_values_window = 1,                  covariates_subgroup_model = c("covariate_sim_normal",                                                "covariate_sim_binary1"),                  conv_pct_criteria = 0.03,                  n_inits = 25 ) set.seed(1234)  ex_aft_fmm <- survFMM(                  model = "AFT-FMM",                  input_df = sim_data,                  weights = "iptw_trim97",                  outc_distribution = "weibull",                  outc_model_time = "time_to_event_days",                  outc_model_status = "event_status",                  outc_model_covars = "tx",                  k = 3,                  starting_values_type = "single_survreg",                  starting_values_window = 1,                  covariates_subgroup_model = c("covariate_sim_normal",                                                "covariate_sim_binary1"),                  conv_pct_criteria = 0.03,                  n_inits = 25 )

title: Fitting Finite Mixture Models for Time-to-Event Endpoints
text: Note: subgroup labels assigned assigned IPCW-FMM AFT-FMM may vary (.e., called “Subgroup 1” IPCW-FMM may called “Subgroup 2” AFT-FMM). list elements returned survFMM. starting_values dataframe starting values used final initial partition. final_outcome_model_1-k outcome model objects subgroup, subgroups 1-k. final_outcome_model_tidy_1-k dataframes final outcome model estimates subgroup, subgroups 1 k. final_outcome_model_cov_mtx_1-k dataframes final outcome model covariance matrices subgroup, subgroups 1 k. subgroup_assn dataframe structured 1 row/observation including subgroup assigned based largest posterior probability (assn_subgroup), well posterior probabilities membership subgroup (posterior_prob1-k). final_df dataframe structured 1 row/observation/potential subgroup. includes posterior probability subgroup membership subgroup, long format (posterior_prob). Users may filter k = assn_subgroup get 1 row/observation/assigned subgroup analysis. log_likelihood_values vector log likelihood values across iterations EM algorithm. convergence_status, convergence_message, convergence_iter vectors indicating whether algorithm converged (0 = converge, 1 = converged), convergence criteria met (log-likelihood percent subjects changing subgroup) reason lack convergence, iteration algorithm stopped.
code: str(ex_aft_fmm, 1) #> List of 21 #>  $ starting_values              : tibble [1 × 9] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ final_outcome_model_1        :List of 17 #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "survreg" #>  $ final_outcome_model_2        :List of 17 #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "survreg" #>  $ final_outcome_model_3        :List of 17 #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "survreg" #>  $ final_outcome_model_tidy_1   : tibble [3 × 7] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ final_outcome_model_tidy_2   : tibble [3 × 7] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ final_outcome_model_tidy_3   : tibble [3 × 7] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ final_outcome_model_cov_mtx_1: tibble [3 × 3] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ final_outcome_model_cov_mtx_2: tibble [3 × 3] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ final_outcome_model_cov_mtx_3: tibble [3 × 3] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ final_subgroup_model         :List of 26 #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr [1:2] "multinom" "nnet" #>  $ final_subgroup_model_tidy    : tibble [6 × 8] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ final_subgroup_model_cov_mtx : num [1:6, 1:6] 7.61e-03 -5.77e-04 -7.55e-03 4.29e-03 -4.97e-05 ... #>  $ subgroup_assn                : tibble [1,500 × 6] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ x1_prev_iter                 : tibble [4,500 × 21] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ final_df                     : tibble [4,500 × 21] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ log_likelihood_values        : num [1:5] -3030 -2968 -2956 -2950 -2948 #>  $ convergence_status           : num 1 #>  $ convergence_message          : chr "Algorithm converged (<=3% records changed assigned subgroup)." #>  $ convergence_iter             : int 5 #>  $ ests                         :'data.frame':   90 obs. of  14 variables:

title: Fitting Finite Mixture Models for Time-to-Event Endpoints
text: Note: subgroup labels assigned assigned IPCW-FMM AFT-FMM may vary (.e., called “Subgroup 1” IPCW-FMM may called “Subgroup 2” AFT-FMM). can first describe number patients assigned subgroup. can explore extent feature interest contributed subgroup membership reviewing latent subgroup membership model. Additionally, can explore whether patient characteristics vary subgroup assignment.
code: tbl_merge(   list(     tbl_summary(ex_ipcw_fmm$subgroup_assn,       include = assn_subgroup     ),     tbl_summary(ex_aft_fmm$subgroup_assn,       include = assn_subgroup     )   ),   tab_spanner = c("**IPCW-FMM**", "**AFT-FMM**") ) %>%   bold_labels() bind_rows(   ex_ipcw_fmm$final_subgroup_model_tidy %>%    filter(!(term %in% c("(Intercept)", "Log(scale)"))) %>%    mutate(model = "IPCW-FMM",          across(.cols = c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high),                 .fns = ~round(exp(.x), 2)),          p.value = format.pval(p.value, digits = 2, eps = 0.001)) %>%    select(model, y.level, term, `Odds Ratio` = estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value) ,   ex_aft_fmm$final_subgroup_model_tidy %>%    filter(!(term %in% c("(Intercept)", "Log(scale)"))) %>%    mutate(model = "AFT-FMM",          across(.cols = c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high),                 .fns = ~round(exp(.x), 2)),          p.value = format.pval(p.value, digits = 2, eps = 0.001)) %>%    select(model, y.level, term, `Odds Ratio` = estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)) %>%    group_by(model) %>%    gt::gt() %>%   gt::tab_style(     style = gt::cell_text(weight = "bold"),     locations = gt::cells_row_groups(groups = everything())   ) tbl_stack(   list(     ex_ipcw_fmm$final_df %>%      # get one row per observation     filter(k == assn_subgroup) %>%      tbl_summary(by = assn_subgroup,                 include = c(covariate_sim_normal,                             covariate_sim_binary1)) %>%      bold_labels() %>%      modify_header(all_stat_cols() ~ "**Subgroup {level}**"),     ex_aft_fmm$final_df %>%      # get one row per observation     filter(k == assn_subgroup) %>%      tbl_summary(by = assn_subgroup,                 include = c(covariate_sim_normal,                             covariate_sim_binary1)) %>%      bold_labels() %>%      modify_header(all_stat_cols() ~ "**Subgroup {level}**")   ),   group_header = c("IPCW-FMM", "AFT-FMM") ) %>%   bold_labels() %>% as_gt() %>%   gt::tab_style(     style = gt::cell_text(weight = "bold"),     locations = gt::cells_row_groups(groups = everything())   )

title: Fitting Finite Mixture Models for Time-to-Event Endpoints
text: subgroups obtained, can examine treatment effects within subgroup whether heterogeneity present: formally test treatment effect heterogeneity, joint test (shown) can performed test H0:βtx1=βtx2=βtx3H_0: \beta_{tx_1} = \beta_{tx_2} = \beta_{tx_3}H1:βtx1≠βtx2 βtx1≠βtx3H_1: \beta_{tx_1} \ne \beta_{tx_2} \text{ } \beta_{tx_1} \ne \beta_{tx_3} βtxk\beta_{tx_k} corresponds treatment term outcome model latent subgroup kk. variance covariance treatment terms across outcome models can estimating bootstrapping: sampling data replacement fitting FMM repeatedly estimate variance covariance outcome model treatment terms across bootstrap replicates.
code: # custom tidier to exponentiate model coefficients custom_tidier <- function(x, ...) broom::tidy(x, ...) %>%    dplyr::filter(!(.data$term %in% c("(Intercept)", "Log(scale)"))) %>%    mutate(across(all_of(c("estimate", "conf.low", "conf.high")), ~exp(.))) # table of outcome models from IPCW-FMM tbl_outc_ipcw_fmm <- tbl_merge(   list(     tbl_regression(ex_ipcw_fmm$final_outcome_model_1, tidy_fun = custom_tidier),     tbl_regression(ex_ipcw_fmm$final_outcome_model_2, tidy_fun = custom_tidier),     tbl_regression(ex_ipcw_fmm$final_outcome_model_3, tidy_fun = custom_tidier)   ),   tab_spanner = c("**Subgroup 1**", "**Subgroup 2**", "**Subgroup 3**") )  # table of outcome models from AFT-FMM tbl_outc_aft_fmm <- tbl_merge(   list(     tbl_regression(ex_aft_fmm$final_outcome_model_1, tidy_fun = custom_tidier),     tbl_regression(ex_aft_fmm$final_outcome_model_2, tidy_fun = custom_tidier),     tbl_regression(ex_aft_fmm$final_outcome_model_3, tidy_fun = custom_tidier)   ),   tab_spanner = c("**Subgroup 1**", "**Subgroup 2**", "**Subgroup 3**") )  # combine tables tbl_stack(list(tbl_outc_ipcw_fmm, tbl_outc_aft_fmm),           group_header = c("IPCW-FMM", "AFT-FMM")) %>%    as_gt() %>%   gt::tab_style(     style = gt::cell_text(weight = "bold"),     locations = gt::cells_row_groups(groups = everything())   )

title: Fitting Finite Mixture Models for Time-to-Event Endpoints
text: Due random initialization starting values initial partitions patients latent subgroups part EM algorithm, setting seed prior calling survFMM() required ensure full reproducibility. recommended n_init least 10 higher. parameter controls number random initial partitions starting values used algorithm initiation, initialization resulting largest log-likelihood selected final model estimates; higher values n_init result stable estimation. small proportion censored patients, IPCW-FMM AFT-FMM may result similar estimates. higher proportions censored patients, AFT-FMM recommended observations directly incorporated model estimation estimation inverse probability censoring weights required. labels applied subgroup (e.g., “Subgroup 1”, “Subgroup 2”, “Subgroup 3”) arbitrary, may vary across times algorithm run. example, one run algorithm, subgroup 1 may smallest treatment effect another run may largest treatment effect. ensure comparably labeled subgroups across multiple runs models, correct_label_switching function can used.

what: Finite Mixture Models for Time-to-Event Endpoints
title: Finite Mixture Models for Time-to-Event Endpoints
text: {survFMM} package fits adaptations finite mixture model time--event endpoints. Finite mixture models developed model continuous discrete values arising separate latent distributions. implemented adaptation modeling approach time--event endpoints, require additional consideration due observations censored therefore observed event time. IPCW-FMM: One adaptation finite mixture continuous outcomes, fit among individuals event correcting censoring bias via supplied inverse probability censoring (IPCW) weights. approach properties described . AFT-FMM second adaptation mixture accelerated failure time models (AFT-FMM), censored observations directly incorporated subgroup-specific outcome models. approach desirable properties censoring rates higher, given censoring process explicitly modeled censored observations directly incorporated log-likelihood. manuscript detailing approach forthcoming.

McLachlan, Sharon X. Lee, Suren . Rathnayake. 2019. Finite Mixture Models. Annual Review Statistics Application. 6:355-378. https://doi.org/10.1146/annurev-statistics-031017-100325

what: Clean subgroup labels for survFMM objects — clean_subgroup_labels
title: Clean subgroup labels for survFMM objects — clean_subgroup_labels
text: common problem finite mixture modeling subgroup labels superficial, e.g., group labeled 'subgroup 1' always correspond subgroup smallest treatment effect. purpose function update subgroup labels order increasing magnitude treatment term subgroup 1 always smallest treatment term subgroup 2 always largest treatment estimate. Note: function currently compatible `save_all_init` = FALSE `survFMM()` call.

title: EM Algorithm for Finite Mixture Models with Survival Endpoints — fmm_em_algorithm
code: fmm_em_algorithm(   input_df,   weights_input,   starting_values_input_df,   k,   outc_model_formula,   outc_model_time,   outc_model_status,   outc_model_covars,   outc_distribution,   covariates_subgroup_model,   n_inits = 5,   tolerance = 0.001,   conv_pct_criteria = -1,   max_iter = 200 )

title: EM Algorithm for Finite Mixture Models with Survival Endpoints — fmm_em_algorithm
text: input_df Input data frame containing 1 row/observation, along observation's event status (0=censored, 1=event) event time variables weights_input Variable inverse probability treatment weights, applicable. IPCW-FMM, supply IPCW. IPCW-FMM also uses IPTW, product ITPW IPCW may supplied. starting_values_input_df Input dataset starting values algorithm k Number subgroups outc_model_formula Formula object subgroup-specific outcome models outc_model_time Variable indicating time event censoring observation. outc_model_status Variable indicating event status time--event outcome. assumed 0 = censored, 1 = event. outc_model_covars Names covariates include outcome models subgroup outc_distribution Outcome distribution subgroup-specific outcome models. Currently allowed values "Weibull" "Log-Normal" (case-sensitive) covariates_subgroup_model Vector covariates include subgroup membership model n_inits Number initial partitions EM algorithm. Default 5. higher number initial partitions may result greater stability estimates. tolerance Convergence criteria change log-likelihood EM algorithm. conv_pct_criteria Convergence criteria percentage observations changing subgroup. Specify -1 use log-likelihood convergence criteria. max_iter Maximum number iterations. Default 200.

title: EM Algorithm for Finite Mixture Models with Survival Endpoints — fmm_em_algorithm

title: Initialize starting values for each initial partition of the EM algorithm call — initialize_starting_values
text: n_inits Number initial partitions k Number subgroups starting_values_type One "single_survreg", "uniform_pct", "non_random_start". "single_survreg" fits single AFT model data generates random starting values based `starting_values_window` parameter initial partition. supplying starting values, randomly generated initial partition. sure set seed top script ensure reproducibility. starting_values_df Optional input dataset starting values algorithm starting_values_window percent margin around starting values. example, starting_values_type = 'single_survreg' starting_values_window = 0.5 means starting values randomly generated uniformly +/- 50 fit. starting_values_type = 'uniform' starting_values_df supplied, starting values generated uniformly input_df Input data frame containing 1 row/observation, along observation's event status (0=censored, 1=event) event time variables outc_model_formula Formula object subgroup-specific outcome models weights_input Variable inverse probability treatment weights, applicable. IPCW-FMM, supply IPCW. IPCW-FMM also uses IPTW, product ITPW IPCW may supplied. outc_distribution Outcome distribution subgroup-specific outcome models. Currently allowed values "Weibull" "Log-Normal" (case-sensitive)

title: Simulated Dataset — sim_data
Simulated Dataset — sim_data
text: data table simulated data : record_id Record ID covariate_sim_normal Simulated covariate following N(0, 1)   distribution covariate_sim_binary1 Simulated binary covariate p=0.5 covariate_sim_binary2 Simulated binary covariate p=0.4 tx Binary treatment indicator latent_subgroup Simulated latent subgroup event_status Event indicator, 0 = censored, 1 = event time_to_event_days Simulated time event censoring iptw_trim97 Inverse probability treatment weights (IPTW), trimmed   97th percentile ipcw_trim97 Inverse probability censoring weights (IPCW), trimmed   97th percentile; populated observations censored iptw_ipcw_trim97 Product IPTW inverse probability censoring weights (IPCW), trimmed   97th percentile; populated observations censored

what: Fit a finite mixture model for survival endpoints — survFMM
title: Fit a finite mixture model for survival endpoints — survFMM
text: function fits adaptations finite mixture model time--event endpoints. One adaptation finite mixture continuous outcomes, fit among individuals event correcting censoring bias via supplied inverse probability censoring (IPCW) weights. approach properties described Unveiling non-small cell lung cancer treatment effect heterogeneity: comparative analysis statistical methods (2025). second adaptation mixture accelerated failure time models (AFT-FMM), censored observations directly incorporated subgroup-specific outcome models.

title: Fit a finite mixture model for survival endpoints — survFMM
code: survFMM(   input_df,   weights_input = NULL,   outc_model_time = NULL,   outc_model_status = NULL,   outc_model_covars = NULL,   outc_distribution = "weibull",   starting_scale_logn = "exp",   covariates_subgroup_model = NULL,   model = "AFT-FMM",   k = 2,   starting_values_type = "single_survreg",   starting_values_window = 1,   starting_values_df = NULL,   n_inits = 5,   tolerance = 0.001,   conv_pct_criteria = -1,   max_iter = 200,   save_all_init = FALSE )

title: Fit a finite mixture model for survival endpoints — survFMM
text: input_df Input data frame containing 1 row/observation, along observation's event status (0=censored, 1=event) event time variables weights_input Variable inverse probability treatment weights, applicable. nothing supplied AFT-FMM, IPTW computed. IPCW-FMM, supply IPCW. IPCW-FMM also uses IPTW, product ITPW IPCW may supplied. outc_model_time Variable indicating time event censoring observation. outc_model_status Variable indicating event status time--event outcome. assumed 0 = censored, 1 = event. outc_model_covars Names covariates include outcome models subgroup. Note covariates continuous form numeric dummy variables. outc_distribution Outcome distribution subgroup-specific outcome models. Currently allowed values "Weibull" "Log-Normal" (case-sensitive) covariates_subgroup_model Names covariates include subgroup membership model. model "AFT-FMM" finite mixture accelerated failure time (AFT) models, "IPCW-FMM" finite mixture continuous distribution weighted inverse probability censoring weights (IPCW). Input case sensitive. Default AFT-FMM. k Number subgroups. Default 2. starting_values_type One "single_survreg", "uniform_pct", "non_random_start". "single_survreg" fits single AFT model data generates random starting values based `starting_values_window` parameter initial partition. supplying starting values, randomly generated initial partition. sure set seed top script ensure reproducibility. Default "single_survreg." starting_values_window percent margin around starting values. example, starting_values_type = 'single_survreg' starting_values_window = 0.5 means starting values randomly generated uniformly around +\/- 50% estimates single survreg model fit. Default 1 (.e., starting values +\/- 100%). starting_values_df Input dataset starting values algorithm n_inits Number initial partitions EM algorithm. Default 5. higher number initial partitions may result greater stability estimates. tolerance Convergence criteria change log-likelihood EM algorithm. Default 0.001. conv_pct_criteria Convergence criteria percentage observations changing subgroup. Specify -1 use log-likelihood convergence criteria. max_iter Maximum number iterations. Default 200. save_all_init Whether results initial partitions saved. Default FALSE.

title: Fit a finite mixture model for survival endpoints — survFMM
text: List results following components: starting_values: Dataframe starting values used algorithm initialization final_outcome_model_1-final_outcome_model_k: Outcome model objects    latent subgroup, 1 k final_outcome_model_tidy_1-final_outcome_model_tidy_k: Tidy dataframe   corresponding outcome model latent subgroup, 1 k final_outcome_model_cov_mtx_1-final_outcome_model_cov_mtx_k: Tibble   covariance matrix corresponding outcome model latent   subgroup, 1 k subgroup_assn: Dataframe containing posterior probability   subgroup membership corresponding assigned subgroup (based maximum   posterior probability) observation final_subgroup_model: Final model subgroup membership final_subgroup_model_tidy: Tidy dataframe corresponding final   model subgroup membership final_df: One observation per record, per subgroup containing   input dataset corresponding prior posterior probabilities. log_likelihood_values: Vector log-likelihood values across   algorithm iterations convergence_status: Numeric convergence status. 0 =   converge, 1 = algorithm converged convergence_message: Message indicating whether algorithm converged convergence_iter: Final iteration algorithm. Either   iteration algorithm achieved convergence, final iteration   following error, maximum number iterations (non-convergence)

title: Fit a finite mixture model for survival endpoints — survFMM
text: Due random initialization starting values initial partitions patients latent subgroups part EM algorithm, setting seed prior calling survFMM strongly recommended ensure full reproducibility.

title: Fit a finite mixture model for survival endpoints — survFMM
code: # Examples using package test data # Example 1 ---------------------------------- # Fit a mixture of accelerated failure time models ex_aft_fmm <- survFMM(                  model = "AFT-FMM",                  input_df = sim_data,                  weights = "iptw_trim97",                  outc_distribution = "weibull",                  outc_model_time = "time_to_event_days",                  outc_model_status = "event_status",                  outc_model_covars = "tx",                  covariates_subgroup_model = "covariate_sim_normal",                  n_inits = 5) #>  ■■■■■■■                           20% |  ETA: 26s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■                     40% |  ETA: 33s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■               60% |  ETA: 34s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■         80% |  ETA: 16s #' # Example 2 ---------------------------------- # Fit a mixture of Weibull models, weighted by the inverse probability of # censoring ex_ipcw_fmm <- survFMM(                  model = "IPCW-FMM",                  input_df = sim_data,                  weights = "iptw_ipcw_trim97",                  outc_distribution = "weibull",                  outc_model_time = "time_to_event_days",                  outc_model_status = "event_status",                  outc_model_covars = "tx",                  covariates_subgroup_model = "covariate_sim_normal",                  n_inits = 5) #>  ■■■■■■■                           20% |  ETA:  1m #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■                     40% |  ETA:  1m #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■               60% |  ETA: 27s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■         80% |  ETA: 15s

title: survFMM 0.1.0
text: Initial package version.
